summary: Underwriting Service — Get Underwriting Decision REST API (4.1)
description:
Implement the GET /api/v1/underwriting/{applicationId} endpoint defined in api-reference.md section 4.1. This epic covers the full vertical slice for the read path: the UnderwritingDecisionResponse DTO, a scaffolded UnderwritingDecisionService returning hardcoded default values matching the api-reference example response, the UnderwritingController wiring the GET endpoint, and a RestControllerAdvice using Spring Boot's built-in ProblemDetail (RFC 7807) for structured error responses on 404 and invalid path variable types.

No custom API error response shape is introduced — error responses use ProblemDetail exclusively. Security enforcement (401/403) is delegated to the API Gateway and Spring Security, which are outside this epic's scope.
userType: loan officer or admin authenticated through the API Gateway
functionality: retrieve the underwriting outcome and review summary for a specific loan application via a stable REST endpoint
benefit: so downstream consumers, the frontend dashboard, and manual reviewers can read the underwriting decision result for a given application without coupling directly to the underwriting database

stories.summary: Create UnderwritingDecisionResponse DTO and scaffold UnderwritingDecisionService
stories.description:
Define the UnderwritingDecisionResponse DTO whose fields match the GET /api/v1/underwriting/{applicationId} example response from api-reference.md section 4.1. Scaffold the UnderwritingDecisionService interface and its initial implementation that returns hardcoded default values matching that example — enabling the controller to be built and integrated against a stable contract before real persistence logic is wired.

Expected default response shape:

```
{
  "applicationId": 50021,
  "decisionStatus": "APPROVED",
  "decisionAt": "2026-03-24T16:02:11Z",
  "decisionReason": "Income and debt ratio within policy thresholds",
  "recommendedAmount": 25000,
  "recommendedTermMonths": 48,
  "manualReviewRequired": false
}
```

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: DTO fields match the api-reference 4.1 example response
acceptanceCriteria.given: the UnderwritingDecisionResponse DTO is instantiated with all fields set
acceptanceCriteria.when: the object is serialised to JSON by Jackson
acceptanceCriteria.then: the output contains applicationId, decisionStatus, decisionAt, decisionReason, recommendedAmount, recommendedTermMonths, and manualReviewRequired with names and types matching the api-reference example

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Scaffold service returns default values for any applicationId
acceptanceCriteria.given: no real persistence is wired and the service implementation is the scaffold
acceptanceCriteria.when: getDecision is called with any applicationId
acceptanceCriteria.then: the returned DTO carries the hardcoded defaults from the api-reference 4.1 example response with applicationId set to the requested value

tasks.description:
Create com.loanflow.underwritingservice.dto.UnderwritingDecisionResponse with the following fields:

- applicationId (long)
- decisionStatus (String)
- decisionAt (Instant)
- decisionReason (String)
- recommendedAmount (BigDecimal)
- recommendedTermMonths (Integer)
- manualReviewRequired (boolean)

Annotate with @Data, @Builder, @NoArgsConstructor, @AllArgsConstructor (Lombok).

Create com.loanflow.underwritingservice.service.UnderwritingDecisionService with a single method:

UnderwritingDecisionResponse getDecision(Long applicationId);

Create com.loanflow.underwritingservice.service.UnderwritingDecisionServiceImpl annotated with @Service, implementing UnderwritingDecisionService.

getDecision must return a UnderwritingDecisionResponse built with:

- applicationId: the incoming applicationId argument
- decisionStatus: "APPROVED"
- decisionAt: Instant.parse("2026-03-24T16:02:11Z")
- decisionReason: "Income and debt ratio within policy thresholds"
- recommendedAmount: new BigDecimal("25000")
- recommendedTermMonths: 48
- manualReviewRequired: false

This scaffold is intentionally replaced when real persistence and decision logic are implemented.

stories.description: Wire the GET /api/v1/underwriting/{applicationId} endpoint defined in api-reference.md section 4.1. The controller delegates to UnderwritingDecisionService and returns 200 OK with the UnderwritingDecisionResponse body. Auth enforcement (401/403) is handled upstream by the API Gateway — no security annotations are added at this stage.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: GET request returns 200 with decision response body
acceptanceCriteria.given: the service is running and UnderwritingDecisionServiceImpl is the active bean
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response is 200 OK with Content-Type application/json and a body matching the api-reference 4.1 example response with applicationId 50021

acceptanceCriteria.given: the controller and exception handler are wired
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response is 400 Bad Request with a ProblemDetail body containing a timestamp property and a detail describing the invalid path variable value

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Unknown applicationId returns 404 ProblemDetail when service throws UnderwritingCaseNotFoundException
acceptanceCriteria.given: the service throws UnderwritingCaseNotFoundException for the requested applicationId
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response is 404 Not Found with a ProblemDetail body containing title Not Found, a detail message referencing the applicationId, and a timestamp property

tasks.description:
Create com.loanflow.underwritingservice.controller.UnderwritingController annotated with @RestController and @RequestMapping("/api/v1/underwriting").

Inject UnderwritingDecisionService via constructor injection.

Expose:

@GetMapping("/{applicationId}")
public ResponseEntity<UnderwritingDecisionResponse> getUnderwritingDecision(@PathVariable Long applicationId)

Delegate to underwritingDecisionService.getDecision(applicationId) and return ResponseEntity.ok(...).

Add a Javadoc comment documenting the auth requirements: LOAN_OFFICER and ADMIN have full access; APPLICANT may view final decision summary only (policy-dependent, enforced by API Gateway).

stories.summary: Add UnderwritingExceptionHandler ControllerAdvice with ProblemDetail error responses
stories.description: Implement a @RestControllerAdvice that produces RFC 7807 ProblemDetail responses for error cases surfaced by the underwriting controller. No custom API error response shape is used — ProblemDetail is the sole error format. Handlers are required for UnderwritingCaseNotFoundException (404) and MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException (400 for non-numeric path variables).

acceptanceCriteria.given: the exception handler is registered and the service throws UnderwritingCaseNotFoundException
acceptanceCriteria.when: the exception propagates to the handler
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response status is 404, the body is a ProblemDetail with title Not Found, detail containing the applicationId, type about:blank, and a timestamp property set to the current instant

acceptanceCriteria.given: a non-numeric value is passed as the applicationId path variable
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response status is 400, the body is a ProblemDetail with title Bad Request, detail identifying the invalid parameter name and value, and a timestamp property

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: No custom error response class is introduced
acceptanceCriteria.given: the exception handler implementation is reviewed
acceptanceCriteria.when: all error response types are inspected
acceptanceCriteria.then: only Spring Boot's built-in ProblemDetail is used — no custom ApiError or ErrorResponse wrapper classes exist

tasks.description:
Create com.loanflow.underwritingservice.exception.UnderwritingCaseNotFoundException extending RuntimeException.

Single constructor: UnderwritingCaseNotFoundException(Long applicationId) with message "Underwriting case not found for application id: " + applicationId.

Create com.loanflow.underwritingservice.exception.UnderwritingExceptionHandler annotated with @RestControllerAdvice.

Handler methods:

1. @ExceptionHandler(UnderwritingCaseNotFoundException.class)
   - Returns ProblemDetail.forStatusAndDetail(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND, ex.getMessage())
   - Sets title to "Not Found", type to URI.create("about:blank"), property "timestamp" to Instant.now()

2. @ExceptionHandler(MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException.class)
   - Returns ProblemDetail.forStatusAndDetail(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "Invalid value '" + ex.getValue() + "' for parameter '" + ex.getName() + "'")
   - Sets title to "Bad Request", type to URI.create("about:blank"), property "timestamp" to Instant.now()

Both methods return ProblemDetail directly — no ResponseEntity wrapper needed as Spring Boot 3.x handles status resolution from ProblemDetail automatically.
